text: Dear colleagues, we'd like to present you the case of a right S3 segmentectomy as we do it at our institution.
We start by doing an intercostal block using 20 ml of Propivacaine 0.75%.
Here we dissect the pleura along the parenchymal margin in order to expose the superior pulmonary vein.
We retract the apex of the right lung cordially in order to expose the pulmonary artery and the right main branches.
In order to gain access to the truncus anterior and the right upper branches, we continue the dissection along the parenchymal margin.
Here we remove several N1 lymph nodes in order to gain access to the important structures.
In order to gain access to the vascular structures we proceed by separating the superior pulmonary vein from the pulmonary artery.
As we can see here this allows us to retract the superior pulmonary vein and to gain access to the truncus anterior.
We are now dissecting the adventitia of the artery for segment 3.
After careful isolation of the segment 3 artery we can now proceed by dividing the artery using a vascular stapler.
In this case we chose to open the fissure before dividing the segment three branches and vein but it is also possible to perform this step at last.
After identifying the artery state dedicated to the middle lobe we can now create a tunnel between the hilum and the fissure.
Here we introduce the bachy forceps between the middle pulmonary vein and the upper pulmonary vein.
As we flip the upper and middle lobe we can exit the tip approximately to the A5 artery.
This maneuver is only necessary because the patient has an incomplete horizontal fissure.
In order to get better orientation we introduce a vessel loop into the created tunnel.
Now we open the fissure using a normal stapler.
As we can see here, the vessel loop can serve as a guide for the stapler.
Now we can safely identify the segment 3 vein and divide it using a vascular stapler.
Before we fire the stapler, we release the tension on the vane in order to create an impervious suture.
We can follow the segment 2 vane in order to safely identify the segment 3 branches.
There is no risk in using a surgical swab to safely expose the branches.
Now we isolate the segment 3 branches in order to divide it.
We use ventilation in order to double check the correct location of the stapler.
We use indo-CNN green in order to identify the margins of the segment 3.
While dividing the parenchyma we pay close attention to resect the bronchial and vascular stumps.
After retrieving the segment 3 in a sterile bag, we check for air tightness.
To check the correct perfusion of the remaining lung we inject another dose of ICG.
Finally, we insert a 24 French chest tube and check for correct lung re-expansion.
